title: SV interfaces fail to synthesize depending on module order
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

A submodule `\submod` takes an interface `\iface` as a port.
A parent module `\top` instantiates both.

When passing the `\top` module to Yosys first, there is no error:

`yosys -f "verilog -sv" -p "read_verilog -icells -lib +/ice40/cells_sim.v; hierarchy -check -top top;" top.sv submod.sv iface.sv`

But when `\submod` comes before `\top`:

`yosys -f "verilog -sv" -p "read_verilog -icells -lib +/ice40/cells_sim.v; hierarchy -check -top top;" submod.sv top.sv iface.sv`

Then Yosys fails with this error:

>5.1. Analyzing design hierarchy..
Top module:  \top
Used module:     \submod
Used module:     \iface
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\submod`.
ERROR: Module `submod` referenced in module `top` in cell `submod_inst` does not have a port named 'ifaceport'.

This can be reproduced with those three files:

>top.sv
```systemverilog
module top(input clk);
    iface #(.YOSYS_BUG_KEEP_IFACE(1)) iface_inst();
    submod submod_inst(.clk, .ifaceport(iface_inst));
endmodule
```
>submod.sv
```systemverilog
module submod(input clk, iface ifaceport);
    assign ifaceport.x = 1;
endmodule
```

>iface.sv
```systemverilog
// We need a dummy parameter when instantiating interfaces or they fail to synthetize
interface iface #(YOSYS_BUG_KEEP_IFACE = 0) ();
    logic x;
endinterface
```

## Expected behavior

The order should not matter.

## Actual behavior

This could have the same root cause as #1360 since I only reproduce it with the ice40 cell libs, but just in case here are the symptoms.

It seems when `\submod` is processed first by the hierarchy pass, `AstModule::reprocess_module` is called on it early which results in the interface's ports being exploded: `submod_inst.ifaceport` is replaced by `submod_inst.ifaceport.x`.
Then when we expand module `\top` it still expects to connect to the un-exploded port (`connect \ifaceport $dummywireforinterface\iface_inst`)

Expanding `\top` first seems to work because it can connect to `top.submod_inst.ifaceport`, before the ports of both modules get exploded.

I haven't looked into it much further than this.
